title: Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple
tagline: Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shasta, located in Kulathupuzha in the Kollam district of Kerala, India.
overview.description: Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shasta, located in Kulathupuzha in the Kollam district of Kerala, India. It is one of the five important Sastha temples in Kerala, the others being Aryankavu, Achankovil, Sabarimala and Kanthamala. The idol there is believed to have been installed by Parashurama and is made up of eight pieces of stone. The temple is known for the Meenoottu (fish feeding) offering.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kulathupuzha Forest Museum
topPlaces.description: Kulathupuzha Forest Museum is a museum in India. It is located in Kulathupuzha in Kollam district of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kulathupuzha
topPlaces.description: Kulathupuzha is a town in the eastern part of the Kollam district of Kerala, India. It is one of the important pilgrim spots linked with the legend of Sabarimala.

topPlaces.name: Thenmala
topPlaces.description: Thenmala is a tourist destination on the eastern side of Kollam district in Kerala, India. It is home to the first ecotourism centre in India.

topPlaces.name: Thenmala Dam
topPlaces.description: The Thenmala Dam also known as Parappar Dam is part of the largest irrigation project in Kerala, India. It is built along Kallada River in Kollam district of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge, also called the "13 Arch Bridge", is a historic British-era structure on the Kollam-Sengottai railway line in India. It is situated at Kazhuthurutty in the Kollam district of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kudukkathu Para
topPlaces.description: Kudukkathu Para is a complex of three rocks situated at Anakkulam in Anchal Forest range and Anchal block Panchayath of Kollam district in Kerala, India, and is a popular tourist spot. The rock structure is 840 metre above sea level, and is developed as an eco-tourism project by the Kerala Forest Department.

topPlaces.name: Vilakkupara
topPlaces.description: Vilakkupara is a village in Punalur Taluk, Kollam District, Kerala State, India. Vilakkupara lies 58 km (36 mi) to the east of Kollam, the District headquarters, and 12 km (7 mi) to the southeast of Punalur on Kollam-Thirumangalam National Highway (N.H-744).

items.note: Kulathupuzha Forest Museum is a museum in India. It is located in Kulathupuzha in Kollam district of Kerala.
Kulathupuzha is a town in the eastern part of the Kollam district of Kerala, India. It is one of the important pilgrim spots linked with the legend of Sabarimala.
Thenmala is a tourist destination on the eastern side of Kollam district in Kerala, India. It is home to the first ecotourism centre in India.
The Thenmala Dam also known as Parappar Dam is part of the largest irrigation project in Kerala, India. It is built along Kallada River in Kollam district of Kerala.

The Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge, also called the "13 Arch Bridge", is a historic British-era structure on the Kollam-Sengottai railway line in India. It is situated at Kazhuthurutty in the Kollam district of Kerala.

Kudukkathu Para is a complex of three rocks situated at Anakkulam in Anchal Forest range and Anchal block Panchayath of Kollam district in Kerala, India, and is a popular tourist spot. The rock structure is 840 metre above sea level, and is developed as an eco-tourism project by the Kerala Forest Department.

Vilakkupara is a village in Punalur Taluk, Kollam District, Kerala State, India. Vilakkupara lies 58 km (36 mi) to the east of Kollam, the District headquarters, and 12 km (7 mi) to the southeast of Punalur on Kollam-Thirumangalam National Highway (N.H-744).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kulathupuzha Sastha Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kulathupuzha Forest Museum, Kulathupuzha, Thenmala, Thenmala Dam, Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge.
